title: IPSec Tunnel Gateway and Security Association Troubleshooting
description: Inspect gateway definitions, IKE phase 1, IPSec phase 2, tunnel inventory, and tunnel mode to isolate the failing VPN layer.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook checks Palo Alto VPN health with #show vpn flow, #show vpn gateway, #show vpn ike-sa, #show vpn ipsec-sa, and #show vpn tunnel.

  1. Establish scope: inspect VPN counters and configured gateways.
  2. Split the fault domain: treat IKE phase 1 and IPSec phase 2 as separate health checks.
  3. Verify tunnel definition: confirm tunnel inventory and mode if configuration mismatch is suspected.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Review VPN counters and gateways
> show vpn flow
> show vpn gateway

# Step 2: Inspect phase 1 and phase 2 state
> show vpn ike-sa
> show vpn ipsec-sa

# Step 3: Verify tunnel inventory and mode
> show vpn tunnel
> set network tunnel ipsec <name_of_tunnel> ipsec-mode [tunnel | transport]
prerequisites: Knowledge of the relevant tunnel or gateway when making targeted checks
detectionNotes: VPN inspection is low-noise management activity and should be protected by strict admin access policy
Configuration changes to tunnel mode should remain under formal change control

steps.name: Query VPN Counters and Gateways
steps.tooltip: This provides the starting point before isolating phase 1, phase 2, or tunnel-definition problems.
steps.description: The operator checks overall VPN counters and the configured gateway list to frame the tunnel investigation.
steps.cli:
> show vpn flow
> show vpn gateway

steps.name: Negotiate IKE Phase 1
steps.tooltip: Phase 1 health must be understood before blaming the tunnel for data-plane failure.
steps.description: The remote peer participates in phase 1 negotiation and the firewall exposes the resulting IKE security-association state.

steps.name: Establish IPSec Phase 2
steps.tooltip: Phase 2 can fail independently even when phase 1 appears healthy.
steps.description: The remote peer and firewall negotiate protected IPSec SAs for data traffic after phase 1 succeeds.

steps.name: Review Tunnel Inventory and Mode
steps.tooltip: Tunnel inventory and mode verification closes the gap between live SA state and local configuration.
steps.description: The firewall exposes the configured auto-key tunnels and their mode so the operator can confirm deployment intent.
steps.cli:
> show vpn tunnel
> set network tunnel ipsec <name_of_tunnel> ipsec-mode [tunnel | transport]

steps.name: Assemble VPN Health Evidence
steps.tooltip: This gives the operator a clear view of which VPN layer is failing.
steps.description: Gateway, phase 1, phase 2, and tunnel-definition evidence are combined into a single VPN health report.
